pr-evidence/native-probe-delegate-factor.gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 reviewer Hypatia the 3rd identified recursion, public constructor compatibility, package importability, dotted/facade alias, and unhashable callable edge cases. Each finding was fixed with regression coverage, and the reviewer approved with no remaining findings.
verification: poetry run pytest tests/test_native_probe_factories.py tests/test_release_bundle.py tests/test_public_package.py -q -> 81 passed
poetry run python -m compileall -q src tests -> passed
poetry run python -m document_kv_cache.pr_evidence --validate-directory pr-evidence --output-json pr-evidence/pr-evidence-sidecar-validation.json -> ok
GPT-5.5 reviewer approved branch native-probe-delegate-factories after findings were resolved
what_changed: Added public vLLM/SGLang native probe delegate environment constants and exported them through document_kv_cache, cachet, and restaurant_kv_serving.
Changed built-in native probe factories to fail closed until the backend package is importable with metadata and a loadable backend-native delegate factory is configured.
Recorded delegate_factory_path in native probe diagnostics and required a non-built-in delegate path for supported release records.
Rejected built-in self-delegation, cross-backend delegation, dotted-path spellings, public facade aliases, and loaded callable aliases that would recurse at runtime.

Added regression coverage for loadable delegates, unloadable delegates, importability mismatches, old positional dataclass construction, alias rejection, validation rejection, and unhashable callable delegates.
pr-evidence/native-probe-delegate-factor.why: Native engine integration needs stable public factory paths for release evidence while allowing real vLLM/SGLang block-manager adapters to live in the target serving environment.
